sentence: Subjective:
The patient, a male, presents with neck pain that began after a car crash in February of this year.
He reports experiencing sharp, incapacitating pain in his neck, which worsens with movement.
He also reports headaches, dizziness, numbness in his left arm and right leg, and muscle spasms throughout his body.
The patient has a history of multiple car crashes, including one in which he underwent surgery, and has been to therapy and a pain clinic.
He is currently taking fentanyl, but has not received a prescription for several weeks.
Past medical history:
The patient has a history of car crashes, including one in which he underwent surgery.
He has also been to therapy and a pain clinic.
Review of systems:
The patient reports headaches, dizziness, numbness in his left arm and right leg, and muscle spasms throughout his body.
Current medications:
Fentanyl (dose and frequency not specified, but patient reports not having received a prescription for several weeks)
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned
Physical exam:
The patient's neck pain is exacerbated by palpation on the bony process and muscle.
He has a moderate range of movement in his neck, but reports pain with bending forward and backward.
He is not experiencing bruising or swelling in his neck at this time.
Test Results:
[NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a neck sprain that can be explained by the multiple car crashes he has experienced.
Problem list: Neck sprain; suspected fracture; unknown.
Plan:
I prescribed robaxin 1500mg, to be taken every 6-8 hours as needed for pain.
I recommended that the patient apply ice to the affected area and perform exercises to maintain range of motion.
I also referred the patient to physical therapy and plan to refer him to pain medicine if necessary.
The patient will undergo an MRI to further evaluate the extent of the injury.
I advised the patient to take a leave of absence from work due to his condition.
Follow-up:
The patient will return for follow-up after the MRI results are available.
